source: AAMC MCAT Official Prep Sample Test Q1-50 (Kass diagnostic export)
section: Chemical and Physical Foundations of Biological Systems

questions.passage: HIV protease is an example of an aspartyl protease...
questions.stem: Compound 1 is used to treat HIV infection based on its ability to act as:
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. An antagonist blocks the biochemical function of the protein it binds to...

questions.stem: How many stereoisomers of Compound 1 exist?
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. There are 5 chiral centers in Compound 1. By application of the 2n rule, there are 32 possible stereoisomers.

questions.stem: Why do kidney stones form in some individuals treated with Compound 1?
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. Kidney stones are effectively a precipitate of CaC2O4(s). Precipitation happens when the reaction quotient Q exceeds the equilibrium constant Ksp.

questions.stem: The peptide bond between which two amino acid residues is cleaved by HIV protease?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. The peptide bond is cleaved between Phe on the N-terminus side and Pro on the C-terminus side.

questions.passage: The central nervous system is an example of an electrical system...
questions.stem: Which image best illustrates the electric field lines between the inside of an axon and the surrounding extracellular solution?
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. By definition, electric field lines exit from positive and enter negative charges. The axon is negatively charged...

questions.stem: One function of the myelin sheath is to:

choices.A: insulate the axon from the surroundings.
choices.B: decrease the radius of the axon.
choices.D: increase the capacitance of the axon.

questions.solution: The correct answer is A. The myelin sheath insulates the axon from the surroundings.

questions.stem: Which substance does Channel X transmit?
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. Sodium cations are small by virtue of their positive charge and low atomic number.

questions.stem: What is the closest distance the electrodes used in an NCV test can be placed on a nerve?
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. According to Figure 2, the shortest duration that can be detected or resolved is 1 msec... 1 msec Ã— 100 m/s = 0.1 m.

questions.stem: What information about an axon is required to calculate the current associated with an NCV pulse?

choices.D: Potential, resistance per unit length, and length

questions.solution: The correct answer is D. The current... can be calculated according to Ohm's law by dividing the potential change by the axon resistance...

questions.passage: Hydrated oxides of carbon and phosphorous...
questions.stem: What was the pH of the unknown aqueous solution?
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. The solution was described in the passage as having a hydronium ion concentration of 1 Ã— 10â€“5. The pH... is 5.

questions.stem: The unknown compound was probably a:
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. The hydrogen ion concentration... is greater than 1 Ã— 10â€“7 but much less than the concentration of the unknown compound itself. This... defines a weak acid.

questions.stem: Two additional compounds were studied: NO2(OH)... and Ni(OH)2... What type of compounds were these?

choices.C: NO2(OH) was a base and Ni(OH)2 was an oxyacid.
choices.D: NO2(OH) was an oxyacid and Ni(OH)2 was a base.

questions.solution: The correct answer is D. The first substance is nitric acid, HNO3... an oxyacid. The latter substance, Ni(OH)2... behavior is typical of substances that feature basic anions.

questions.stem: The central atom, A, of the unknown compound was most likely:

choices.A: an alkali or alkaline earth metal.

questions.solution: The correct answer is C. The compound produces an acidic solution when dissolved. Compounds... are acids if A is a non-metal.

questions.stem: Squalene is produced in the human body as a precursor to which important class of compounds?
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. Squalene is a precursor of the fused four-ring system common to cholesterol and all of the steroid hormones.

questions.stem: What is the concentration of Clâ€“ ions in a 0.1 M solution of calcium chloride?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. The formula for calcium chloride is CaCl2 and it produces twice as many Clâˆ’ ions as Ca2+ ions in solution when it dissolves.

questions.stem: Addition of which disaccharide to a solution of Ag2O in NH3(aq) will NOT result in the deposition of shiny silver mirror?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. Sucrose does NOT possess a hemiacetal functional group and thus cannot ring open to form an aldehyde that will reduce Ag+.

questions.stem: What is the net charge of the peptide Argâ€“Alaâ€“Pheâ€“Leu at pH 8?
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. There is one basic residue, Arg, providing a net +1 charge.

questions.stem: Researchers used reverse-phase HPLC... Which statement accurately describes this process?

choices.A: Increasing polarity of mobile phase will decrease retention time of Compound 1...
choices.C: Decreasing affinity of Compound 1... will increase retention time...
choices.D: Compound 2 will elute first because it does not interact as favorably...

questions.solution: The correct answer is D. Because of its polar hydroxymethyl group, Compound 2 will interact more weakly than Compound 1 with the nonpolar stationary phase, and thus Compound 2 will elute first.

questions.stem: Which data suggest that differences... are NOT due to changes in binding affinity?

choices.B: The KM values for the variant enzymes do not differ significantly...
choices.C: Amino acid substitutions do not change binding pocket...

questions.solution: The correct answer is B. Because binding affinity directly correlates with KM, the binding affinity... is essentially the same...

questions.stem: If data... were displayed in a Lineweaverâ€“Burk plot, the plot would show that the y-intercept:

choices.A: is the same for all three variants.
choices.B: is the same for CYP2C9*1 and CYP2C9*2...
choices.C: is the same for CYP2C9*2 and CYP2C9*3...
choices.D: is different for all three variants.

questions.solution: The correct answer is D. In a Lineweaverâ€“Burk plot, the y-intercept is 1/Vmax. Because... different Vmax value, they will have different y-intercepts.

questions.stem: In this reaction, NADPH functions as:
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. NADPH loses 2 electrons and a proton to become NADP+. Although NADPH is itself oxidized, its role is to transfer these electrons to O2...

questions.stem: What type of reaction is Reaction 1?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. Atoms involved in Reaction 1 change oxidation states... Mg changes from 0 to +2.

questions.stem: Suppose... level of aqueous solution were 26 cm higher inside buret... pressure of gas inside... would be:
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. The level of liquid inside is higher... air pressure outside is higher than pressure inside.

questions.stem: How should the ideal gas equation be rearranged... to calculate the gas constant?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. Rearranging... to isolate R gives R = PV/nT.

questions.stem: What was the major goal of the experiment in the passage?

choices.C: Determine measured variables of Ideal Gas Law

questions.solution: The correct answer is C. The experiment was designed to measure three variables... (P, V, and T)... so that the gas constant R can be calculated.

questions.stem: The average human body contains approximately 5 liters of blood... what is the total mass of blood?
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. The mass of blood equals the blood density 1060 kg/m3 = 1.06 g/cm3 multiplied by the blood volume of 5000 cm3 that is 5.30 kg.

questions.stem: In order to image this motion, the ultrasound examination devices requires what minimal information?

choices.A: Speeds of sound and moving object
choices.B: Speed of sound, frequencies emitted and observed
choices.C: Speeds of sound and moving object, frequencies emitted and observed
choices.D: Speeds of sound and moving object, frequencies and wavelengths

questions.solution: The correct answer is B. The Doppler effect relates the frequency... to the frequency... when the source is stationary, the speed of the source, and the speed of the detector.

questions.stem: Which... explains why the boiling point of HF is abnormally high?

choices.C: HF affected by hydrogen-bonding interactions to much greater degree...

questions.solution: The correct answer is C. Hydrogen bonding is a special type of intermolecular force... causes substances... to have higher than expected boiling points.

questions.stem: The principal quantum number is a measure of which of the following?

choices.A: Approximate radial size of an electron cloud
choices.B: Approximate shape of an electron cloud

questions.solution: The correct answer is A. The principal quantum number n is most closely associated with the potential energy... also true that n is associated with the radial â€œsizeâ€ of the electron cloud.

questions.stem: Metal ions present in plasma are expected to:

choices.A: increase amount of CPFX bound to BSA.
choices.B: decrease amount of CPFX bound to BSA.

questions.solution: The correct answer is B. Based on data in Table 2, metal ions decrease binding of CPFX to BSA by 13â€“36%.

questions.stem: Which naturally occurring biological molecule would be most likely to bind in the same site as ibuprofen?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. Like ibuprofen, palmitoleic acid has a hydrophobic part (its long carbon chain) and a polar carboxylic acid.

questions.stem: Based on data in Table 1, the binding site of CPFX on BSA was determined to be:
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. Warfarin competes for binding... in the amount of more than 50%. Therefore, the binding... occurs primarily at Site I.

questions.stem: Sites I and II are most likely lined with the side chains of which amino acids?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. Because Sites I and II bind to anionic and hydrophobic ligands, the amino acid side chains should be cationic (R)... and hydrophobic (L).

questions.stem: What is the approximate density of the wire at 473 K?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. Density is the ratio of mass to volume, which in this case is 4 Ã— 10âˆ’3 kg / 5 Ã— 10âˆ’7 m3 = 8000 kg/m3.

questions.stem: Which of the following graphs best illustrates the relationship between T and R?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. Resistivity is linearly proportional to temperature. Resistance increases with the increase in temperature.

questions.stem: What is the minimum amount of heat energy required to increase T from 373 K to 573 K?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. The heat q required is given by the relation q = mCÎ”T... q = (4 Ã— 10âˆ’3 kg)(460 J/kgÂ·K)(200 K) = 368 J.

questions.stem: During Trial 5... How did the current through the circuit change?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. Resistance is directly proportional to temperature... Since temperature increases... resistance will increase... From Ohmâ€™s Law, current decreases...

questions.stem: What is the expected 14C labeling pattern found in isopentenyl phosphate?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. The fate of the labeled carbon atoms... must be followed... C2, C4, and C5 will be labeled.

questions.stem: Mevalonate exists in equilibrium with Compound X... What is the structure of Compound X?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. Because mevalonic acid contains both â€“CO2H and â€“OH groups, the open chain acid... will be in equilibrium with the corresponding cyclic ester (mevalonolactone).

questions.stem:
Mevalonate... is expected to display prominent absorption in which region(s) of its IR spectrum?
- I. 3200â€“3500 cmâ€“1
- II. 1700â€“1750 cmâ€“1
- III. 1580â€“1610 cmâ€“1
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. Mevalonic acid contains both â€“CO2H and â€“OH groups... C=O stretching frequency falls in 1700â€“1750 cmâˆ’1, whereas Oâ€“H stretching frequency... in 3200â€“3500 cmâˆ’1.

questions.stem: What factor explains how a single stereoisomer is formed in Reaction 2?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. An enzyme is a chiral catalyst capable of preferentially forming one enantiomer...

questions.stem: Which substance is NOT a product of squalene metabolism?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. Not being a steroid, glucose is not a product of squalene metabolism.

questions.stem: Myopia... The type of lens required is:
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. A diverging lens spreads out light before it reaches the crystalline lens.

questions.stem: What procedure can be used to remove the water from the frozen sample?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. Sublimation of the water under reduced pressure will keep the mixture cold and will maintain the protein in its native state.

questions.stem: Which structure most likely corresponds to amygdalin?
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. Acetal and cyanohydrin functional groups will hydrolyze in H2SO4(aq).

questions.stem: The density of a human body is proportional to:
questions.solution: The correct answer is A. According to Archimedes' principle... body density is then proportional to the ratio between Wair and Wair â€“ Ww.

questions.stem: What is the net charge of both pantothenate and phosphopantothenate... at pH 7?
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. Carboxylic acid of pantothenate will become carboxylate (â€“1)... phosphate group of phosphopantothenate will lose two hydrogen ions (â€“2) and carboxylic acid will lose one... net charge of â€“3.

questions.stem: What are the components that comprise Coenzyme A?
questions.solution: The correct answer is D. These components are all found in the structure of Coenzyme A.

questions.stem: The stabilization of pantothenate in PanK3 is most likely due to an active site:
questions.solution: The correct answer is C. The passage states that binding is stabilized by... a carboxylate group of PanK3. The side chain of aspartate contains a carboxylate group.

questions.stem: Which two amino acids contribute to the stabilization of ADP binding described in the passage?
questions.solution: The correct answer is B. As basic amino acids, both His 11 and Arg27 could be positively charged... and create electrostatic attractive forces with the phosphate groups of ADP.
